key_facts:
Known issues and limitations

 
 Tiering isn't yet supported in a premium block blob storage account.
A lifecycle management policy can't be used to change the tier of a blob that uses an encryption scope to the archive tier.

By using blob lifecycle management, customers can proactively optimize costs by implementing rule-based policies that automatically transition data to cooler tiers or expire it when it's no longer needed.

With the lifecycle management policy, you can:

 
 Transition current versions of a blob, previous versions of a blob, or blob snapshots to a cooler storage tier if these objects aren't accessed or modified for a period of time, to optimize for cost.
You must define at least one action per rule such as changing the blob tier to the cool tier or deleting blobs.

Policy execution

 When you add or edit the rules of a lifecycle policy, it can take up to 24 hours for changes to go into effect and for the first execution to start.

If you delete a policy, then no new policy runs are scheduled, but if a run is already in progress, that run continues until it completes and you're billed for any actions that are required to complete the run.
